critic_raw:
ASSESSOR 1:
Verdict: critique_wins

Three compounding methodological flaws inflate the AUROC advantage: (1) best-score-over-6-hour-window for ML vs. single triage point for qSOFA — this gives the ML model a time window selection advantage; (2) lactate and WBC not available at triage but included in the ML model's features during the window, giving an information advantage; (3) qSOFA is a triage tool, the ML model is a surveillance tool — comparing them with qSOFA at triage and ML over a 6-hour window conflates different clinical tasks. The 0.15 AUROC gap overstates the true comparative advantage.
defender_raw:
ASSESSOR 2:
Verdict: critique_wins

The time horizon asymmetry is the most damaging issue. The ML model is evaluated at its retrospectively best point in the window; qSOFA is evaluated once at arrival. This is not a fair head-to-head. Additionally, the purpose mismatch (triage vs. surveillance) means the paper is comparing the wrong baseline for its deployment claim. The AUROC gap would require a methodologically corrected comparison before supporting a prospective trial.

ASSESSOR 3:
Verdict: critique_wins

All three issues are well-documented in the paper's own methods. The feature availability note (lactate/WBC not always at arrival but usually available within hours) directly supports the information-advantage criticism. The triage documentation note for qSOFA directly supports the purpose mismatch criticism. These are the paper's own words. The AUROC gap of 0.15 is inflated.
adjudication_raw:
Unanimous critique_wins across all three assessors. All three independently identified the same set of issues and reached the same verdict. The issues are not empirically uncertain — they are structural design choices documented in the paper's own methods section. No empirical test would resolve them; the experiment would need to be re-run with matched evaluation designs. ETD constraint applied: these are not genuine but empirically resolvable issues — they are methodology flaws that would require a redesigned study.

Verdict: critique_wins.
